text.title: Instagram Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Instagram Basics methodology + two self-routing anchors. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Allocate ≥80% of weekly content effort to Reels. Stories take ≤15%, Feed posts ≤5%. Reels with hook score < 4/5 are not shipped.

rationale.source: Internal SMM audits Q1 2026; Meta engagement reports 2024-2026

rule.rationale: Reels deliver ~2× the reach of photos by pushing to non-followers. Accounts that ignore Reels get ~1/4 the reach of Reels-led accounts. The first 1.5s decides reach — rate hooks explicitly to enforce quality discipline.

rule.statement: Every Reel MUST include on-screen text covering the key claim. Voiceover-only Reels are rejected before publish.

rationale.source: Meta product analytics; published mute-watch stats

rule.rationale: ~80% of Instagram viewers watch muted. Voiceover-only scripts fail to communicate to the majority of viewers.

rule.statement: Use 3-5 hashtags per post, mixed tiers (small / medium / large). Never request 30 hashtags from an agent or pad a caption.
rule.rationale: Meta favors topic understanding over hashtag density. Over-indexing on hashtags underperforms and looks spammy; agent prompts that demand 30 hashtags yield stale tag lists.

rule.statement: The bio MUST follow the three-line formula: line 1 = what you do for whom; line 2 = credibility/proof; line 3 = CTA. Bios longer than 160 chars are rejected.

rationale.source: Internal SMM audits; Instagram Business docs

rule.rationale: Visitors decide to follow in under 3 seconds. A three-line bio that answers "what, why-trust, what-next" converts profile visits to followers more reliably than a clever but ambiguous bio.

rule.statement: Cross-posted Reels MUST have the TikTok watermark stripped (or be re-edited natively). Watermarked Reels are flagged at QA and not shipped.
rule.rationale: Instagram suppresses reach for Reels that contain the TikTok watermark. Cross-posted content must be re-edited for Instagram's format.

rule.statement: DM automation MUST go through an approved partner (ManyChat / Messenger Platform). Unofficial DM-bot APIs are forbidden — they get accounts suspended.
rule.rationale: Instagram restricts DM automation to approved partners. Unapproved bots get accounts flagged or suspended; that risk dwarfs any reach gain.

rule.statement: Every draft MUST carry a content-pillar tag (Opinion / Value / Question / Personal / Curated). Pillar-less drafts are rejected. Cap agent-generated posts at 50% of weekly volume.
rule.rationale: Without pillar tags, agents drift toward whatever style won last week. The other 50% must come from the operator's lived context — algorithm rewards authentic conversation, not publishing volume.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold (audience overlaps Instagram demographics, team can shoot/edit Reels, brand voice allows visual conversational register), execute the daily content-pack ideation + ship procedure from `04-procedure.xml`.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's affirmative leaf.

rule.statement: If any Skip-If condition is present (B2B enterprise audience, no video capacity, text-heavy brand voice, heavily-regulated industry), do NOT apply this methodology. Route to `growth-linkedin-strategy` or a text-first channel methodology instead.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's skip leaf.
